description: Migration converts an existing contact scalar email into one default email row and removes the scalar email column.
Migration skips contacts with null email and does not create empty email rows.
Database uniqueness rejects two contacts in the same tenant using the same normalized email address.
Database allows the same normalized email address to exist in different tenants.
Contact-model validation accepts a single default canonical email row.
Contact-model validation rejects email collections with no default row when rows are present.
Contact-model validation rejects email collections with more than one default row.
Contact-model validation rejects duplicate custom email labels within a contact payload when labels normalize to the same value.
Contact-model validation rejects malformed email addresses in email rows.
Contact create persists multiple email rows and derives `default_email_address` and `default_email_type` correctly.
Contact update replaces previous email rows, keeps one default, and rehydrates the derived default summary correctly.
Contact lookup by any stored email row returns the owning contact.
Contact search by email matches secondary email rows and returns the contact with its default email summary.
Custom email label suggestions list tenant-scoped labels currently used across contacts.
Unused custom email label definitions can be identified after the last referencing row is removed.
ContactEmailAddressesEditor normalizes draft rows so exactly one default row remains selected.
ContactEmailAddressesEditor compacts empty rows before submit.
ContactEmailAddressesEditor supports canonical label selection and custom label entry with suggestions.
QuickAddContact saves a normalized multi-email payload and blocks submit when email-row validation fails.
ContactDetailsEdit saves the normalized email collection and no longer depends on a scalar contact email input.
Contact detail view renders all email rows and marks the default row distinctly.
Contacts list renders the derived default email summary and not a scalar database email field.
ClientContactsList renders the derived default email summary for each contact.
ContactPicker search results show and resolve by default email while matching secondary email rows.
Shared inbound email service resolves a contact from a secondary email row and returns both matched sender email and default email summary.
Inbound email processing preserves contact authorship on exact sender-email match from a non-default email row.
Inbound email processing uses the contact default email when building outbound follow-up context after matching a secondary sender email.
Workflow `findContactByEmail` resolves contacts through stored email rows instead of scalar contact email.
Workflow/runtime contact output schema includes `email_addresses`, `default_email_address`, and `default_email_type`.
Email-processing workflow mapping distinguishes matched sender email from default outbound email.
Ticket email subscriber sends requester notifications to the default contact email after contact resolution.
Ticket email subscriber handles contacts with secondary matched email rows without sending to the secondary address unless it is also default.
Survey sending resolves the contact default email and fails clearly when the contact has no email rows.
Invoice sending resolves billing contact email through the contact default email before falling back to billing_email.
Watch-list contact selection uses the resolved default email from the chosen contact.
Portal invitation sends to the contact default email and updates when the contact default changes before a new invite is created.
Portal recovery contact checks resolve against any stored contact email row.
Contact-driven registration helpers resolve a contact from any stored email row and use the default email for invite/login semantics.
REST contact create schema accepts `email_addresses` and rejects legacy scalar contact email payloads.
REST contact response schema returns multi-email data plus derived default email summary.
Contact API search/filter by email matches secondary email rows.
Ticket API response contact-email summary is derived from the contact default email row.
n8n contact create/update helper parsing supports multi-email payloads and validates row structure before request dispatch.
n8n outbound contact payload generation includes normalized `email_addresses` rows in requests.
Integration contact lookup actions resolve contacts through stored email rows and remain unique per tenant.
Existing factories and shared helpers build contacts with `email_addresses` rather than scalar email.
DB-backed happy-path integration covers create -> update -> lookup-by-secondary-email -> default-email readback for a contact.
DB-backed guard integration rejects updating a contact to use an email address already assigned to another contact in the same tenant.
